_doc: Per-state licensing requirements for life / health / annuity / mortgage_protection. Every cell starts as research_pending=true. Populate via cited research batch (pattern: migration 0059b — source_url + source_quote on every value, omit rather than invent). DO NOT hand-edit invented values. Schema is frozen 2026-06-02 — bump _schema_version on shape changes.

_lines.label: Life
_lines.notes: Term, whole, IUL, UL, final expense

_lines.label: Health
_lines.notes: Med Supp, MAPD, ACA, dental, vision, group health

_lines.label: Annuity
_lines.notes: Some states fold annuity authority into Life — others require separate. Verify per state.

_lines.label: Mortgage Protection
_lines.notes: Sold under Life LoA; not a separate license — but check state-specific marketing-name rules

_cell_schema.research_pending: boolean — true until a cited row is filled in
_cell_schema.prelicense_hours_required: integer hours; null if state requires no formal pre-licensing course
_cell_schema.prelicense_required_course: boolean — does state mandate a state-approved pre-licensing course
_cell_schema.approved_course_vendors: array of { name, url, source_url } — state-approved pre-licensing providers
_cell_schema.exam_vendor: string enum: PSI | PEARSON_VUE | PROMETRIC | EXAMFX | OTHER
_cell_schema.fingerprint_code: string — the agency/ORI/service code applicants give the fingerprint vendor
_cell_schema.ce_hours_per_cycle: integer continuing-education hours per renewal cycle
_cell_schema.reciprocity_notes: string — does this state grant non-resident license freely?
_cell_schema.source_url: string — citable URL the values trace to (DOI, NIPR, statute)
_cell_schema.source_quote: string — verbatim quote from source_url proving the value

_step_by_step_template.title: Confirm eligibility
_step_by_step_template.what: Age 18+, SSN, US resident or work-authorized, no disqualifying criminal history (varies by state).

_step_by_step_template.title: Complete pre-licensing
_step_by_step_template.what: If state requires it, enroll in an approved course and complete required hours per line. Skip if state waives.

_step_by_step_template.title: Schedule + take state exam
_step_by_step_template.what: Register with the state's exam vendor (PSI / Pearson VUE / Prometric). Pay exam fee. Take exam; pass at state's threshold.

_step_by_step_template.title: Submit fingerprints
_step_by_step_template.what: If required, schedule via the state's fingerprint vendor using the state-issued service code. Pay processing fee.

_step_by_step_template.title: Apply for license
_step_by_step_template.what: Submit application via NIPR (nipr.com) or state DOI portal. Pay application fee. Upload exam result + fingerprint clearance.

_step_by_step_template.title: Get appointed
_step_by_step_template.what: License is dormant until at least one carrier appoints the producer. Use Repflow → Carriers tab to start carrier-appointment requests.

_step_by_step_template.title: First sale + CE planning
_step_by_step_template.what: Calendar renewal date and CE hours. Set Repflow reminders for the renewal cycle.

_research_batch_instructions: Populate via a parallel research-agent batch (mirror migration 0059b methodology). Each agent gets one (state, line) pair and the cell schema above. Agents MUST either fill every required field with source_url + source_quote — OR set research_pending=true with a brief reason. Inventing values = automatic reject. Cited sources should be NAIC, NIPR, the state's DOI producer page, or state statute. Avoid third-party blogs (Kaplan, ExamFX marketing pages, etc.) as primary sources — they're OK as secondary corroboration but not as source_url.

annuity.license_type_note: Fixed annuities are sold under the Life line of authority — no separate annuity producer license in this state.
annuity.reciprocity_notes: Fixed annuities sold under the Life LoA; no separate annuity license. NAIC Model 275 best-interest 4-hr one-time training adopted.
annuity.source_quote: All 50 states have adopted the NAIC Model #275 best-interest standard for annuity sales; producers must complete a one-time 4-credit annuity training course approved by the Department of Insurance. AL is among the adopting jurisdictions.

annuity.reciprocity_notes: Fixed annuities sold under the Life LoA; no separate annuity license. NAIC Model 275 best-interest 4-hr one-time training adopted. Newly added from 'no requirement' under the prior 2010 model.
annuity.source_quote: All 50 states have adopted the NAIC Model #275 best-interest standard for annuity sales; producers must complete a one-time 4-credit annuity training course approved by the Department of Insurance. AR is among the adopting jurisdictions.

annuity.license_type_note: Fixed annuities are sold under the Life line of authority — no separate annuity producer license in this state.
annuity.reciprocity_notes: Fixed annuities sold under the Life-Only LoA; no separate annuity license. CA requires 8 hours initial CA-specific annuity training before solicitation (CIC §1749.8); 4 hours each subsequent license term. SB 263 added 8-hr Annuity Suitability & Best Interest course effective Jan 1, 2025 (existing producers had until Jul 1, 2025).
annuity.source_quote: Section 1749.8 of the California Insurance Code states that any life agent who sells annuities must complete eight hours of approved annuity training prior to soliciting any individual consumers. A four-hour California-specific annuity training course is required each subsequent license term in which the agent sells an annuity.

annuity.reciprocity_notes: Fixed annuities sold under the Life Accident & Health LoA; no separate annuity license. NY has its own best-interest rule — Regulation 187 / 11 NYCRR 224 — broader than NAIC Model 275 (covers life insurance too). Effective Aug 1, 2019 for annuities, Feb 1, 2020 for life. Approved Reg 187 product/best-interest training required.
annuity.source_quote: 11 NYCRR 224 (Insurance Regulation 187): Suitability and Best Interests in Life Insurance and Annuity Transactions. The new regulation became effective August 1, 2019 with respect to annuity transactions and February 1, 2020 with respect to life insurance policies.
